recommendations:
1- Transition from Engagement to "Co-Creation"

The current strategy focuses on "Community Engagement" and "Ask anything" sessions. To strengthen loyalty further, the brand should involve the audience in the content creation process.

2- Hyper-Localize Cultural Content

The Q1 plan mentions "Arabi" and "Chapathi," suggesting a focus on specific regional markets.

  • Recommendation: Develop a mini-series of "Regional Favorites" that showcases Lino Lada paired with local breads or traditional morning drinks beyond just the mentioned "Crunchy Chapathi".
  • Impact: This makes the "everyday essential" positioning feel more personal and relevant to the diverse consumer base.

 

3-. Standardize Visual Overlays for Scannability

The strategy mentions using "Text overlay 3-4 words".

  • Recommendation: Create a standardized "Q1 Visual Kit" where every routine-based post uses the same font and color treatment for these overlays.
  • Impact: This creates a cohesive look that allows users to instantly recognize a "Lino Lada Routine" post while scrolling, reinforcing the "Morning Routine" brand association.

recommendations:
Note:

A surge in Facebook views was recorded in November, directly attributed to the active campaign flight during that period.

 

 

1- Evolution of Visual Storytelling

While maintaining the "clean and airy" aesthetic, the agency suggests a shift in visual pacing for the new year.

  • The "New Year Routine" Aesthetic: Move from the "cozy winter" cues of December to high-energy, bright, and minimalist visuals that reflect a "fresh start".
  • User-Centric Realism: Increase the use of "real-life Jordan moments" by featuring Alkalive in recognizable, premium locations like local boutique gyms, business districts, and wellness cafes.

 

2- Conversion-Focused Educational Content

To meet the objective of "driving consumer conversion through scientific differentiation," the agency recommends a "Show, Don't Just Tell" approach.

  • Live Demonstration: Conduct a live or recorded pH test comparison on Stories to visually substantiate the pH 8–8.5 advantage over regular bottled water.
  • Mineral Bioavailability Series: Use carousels to break down the "rich minerals" mentioned in the plan, explaining exactly why they lead to faster absorption and better recovery for active individuals.

 

3- Community and Trust Building

Building on the "Trust & Transparency" pillar, the agency recommends moving beyond one-way broadcasting.

  • Interactive "Hydration Check" Stories: Utilize poll and slider stickers daily to keep Alkalive top-of-mind during the workday, aligning with the goal of being an "essential part of an aspirational lifestyle".
  • Expert Collaborations: Partner with local Jordanian nutritionists or fitness coaches to provide guest "takeovers," lending third-party credibility to the brand's scientific claims.

recommendations:
December marked our first month of management, establishing Q1 2026 baselines for all platforms. Our content strategy centred on Winter Camp promotion, educational insights, and community engagement, resulting in significant growth in visibility and reach on Facebook, Instagram, and LinkedIn. While our TikTok presence launched in mid-December, it followed a conservative growth and reach gained +2% in a short period, showing promising early engagement for high-energy clips.

 

  • Community-building content (team challenges, parent testimonials) effectively drives engagement.
  • Reels of volleyball challenges/kids’ fun moments and participation drove the highest engagement and reach.
  • Coach-led clips had high save/share potential.
  • Maintain evergreen pillars, push campaigns per month, and strengthen LinkedIn/Facebook engagement.

 

Platform Summary:

Instagram: Views up by +16.1%, Engagement up by+130.8% compared to previous analytics.

Facebook: Views up by +168.9%, Reach up by +328.4%, indicating higher visibility compared to historical baseline.

TikTok: Posting started on mid-December (3rd week), reach gained +2% in a short period.

LinkedIn: Views up by +120.4%, Engagement up by +1,375%, indicating strong response to educational and coach-focused posts.

 

Notes:

We will be using the results from December 2025 analytics as the official baseline for measuring all 2026 social media KPIs as it represents the first month aligned with current strategy, campaigns, and content pillars. Previous 6-month data will still be referenced as historical context to provide trend insights without skewing current strategy impact.

 

Using December as baseline ensures accurate, fair, and actionable measurement of engagement, reach, views, and followers.
title: Esperia Volleyball Academy - December 2025 - Social Media Marketing Report

recommendations:
Key Trends & Insights

  • Growth Leader: Facebook continues to outperform other platforms with strong gains across reach (+64%) and profile visits (+48%), making it our top-performing channel.

  • Follower Growth: All platforms showed positive follower growth, indicating long-term potential for loyalty and audience retention.

  • Visibility Drops: TikTok experienced a significant decline in reach and impressions (↓73%), despite strong follower growth (+9.88%). This is likely due to a temporary pause in posting earlier in the month and some content requiring more time to gain traction.

  • Platform Scale: TikTok still leads in absolute reach and impressions. Instagram maintains the highest follower count, while LinkedIn lags behind in both growth and engagement.


Strategic Recommendations


1. Double Down on Facebook

(High Priority – Allocate 40% of Resources)

  • Organic performance is climbing steadily across all metrics.

  • Continue content frequency, mix educational + community-driven posts.

  • Test targeted boosts on high-performing posts to extend reach.

2. Stabilize and Optimise Instagram

(Medium Priority – Allocate 30%)

  • Growth in followers (+2.72%) and profile visits (+6.51%) is promising.

  • Declining reach suggests content isn’t resonating as strongly.

  • Actions:
    Prioritise Reels, carousels, and story interactions.
    Analyse best-performing posts and replicate format/themes.
    Schedule consistently and allow new content time to perform.

3. Investigate & Pivot TikTok

(Medium Priority – Allocate 20%)

  • Despite the drop in visibility, high follower growth shows strong brand retention.

  • Actions:
    Focus on shorter, high-trend videos, use duets/stitches.
    Reduce post volume to avoid fatigue; prioritise quality over quantity.
    Review competitors and top trends to identify what’s working algorithmically.

4. Platform Alignment Strategy

  • Tailor content to each platform’s strength:
    Facebook: Community, value-driven content, updates.
    Instagram: Aesthetic visuals, interactive Stories, reels.
    TikTok: Viral, snappy, personality-led content.
    LinkedIn: Thought leadership, B2B, announcements.

  • Reuse top-performing content across platforms with format adaptation to maximise efficiency and visibility.
